task_completion.description: Verify that a timed SRT closed caption file was created in the output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing an .srt file in /home/ga/Documents/output/ or a text/subtitle editor showing the file with proper SRT formatting (sequence numbers, timestamps, and transcript text).

task_completion.description: Verify that a hardsubbed video copy with burned-in captions was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing a new video file in /home/ga/Documents/output/ indicating hardsubs, or a video player playing the output video with visible burned-in text on the video frames.

task_completion.description: Verify that a 480p resolution version of the video was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing a distinct video file in /home/ga/Documents/output/ for the 480p version, or a video properties/metadata window showing the resolution is scaled down to 480p (e.g., 854x480 or 640x480).

task_completion.description: Verify that section thumbnail images were extracted from the video.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing one or more image files (e.g., .jpg, .png) in /home/ga/Documents/output/ that display frames from the lecture video.

task_completion.description: Verify that a manifest file was created in the Documents directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing manifest.txt in /home/ga/Documents/ or a text editor open with the manifest file listing the created deliverables.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually processed the videos rather than just copying or renaming the original file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Output video files should have different file sizes or visible differences (like burned-in subtitles or lower resolution) compared to the original lecture_recording.mp4. Look for usage of video editing software or ffmpeg commands.

integrity.description: Ensure the SRT file is genuinely formatted and not just a raw copy-paste of the transcript text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SRT file must contain standard SRT timestamp formatting (e.g., 00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:05,000) rather than just plain text paragraphs.

integrity.description: Ensure the output files are not empty dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: File manager should show non-zero file sizes for the videos and images, or the files should be visibly opened in a media viewer/player.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires generating specific accessibility deliverables from synthetic ffmpeg test sources. The agent must create an SRT file, a hardsubbed video (captions burned into the video track), a 480p scaled video, and thumbnail images, all saved to /home/ga/Documents/output/. A manifest.txt file must also be created in /home/ga/Documents/. Verify that actual subtitle creation, burning, scaling, and snapshot capture occurred, rather than just file renaming.
